frameworks.name: incremental Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: observer updates beliefs sequentially over orientation estimates across trials

frameworks.name: mixture model of past states
frameworks.one_line: internal representation as weighted mixture of previously encountered states

frameworks.name: visual short-term memory
frameworks.one_line: recency bias attributed to representations held in VSTM

core_question: What internal model explains why human observers exhibit visual recency bias even when past events are uninformative about the future?
key_assumptions: human estimation can be framed as incremental Bayesian inference
internal representations of past trials carry forward into current estimate
recency bias persists despite past events being uninformative
manipulations: orientation estimation task across sequential trials
candidate Bayesian models compared in their ability to reproduce recency bias
key_findings: recency bias persists in orientation estimation even when past events are uninformative
only a Bayesian model that weights a mixture of past states reproduces the observed recency bias
interpretations: the mixture-over-past-states structure reflects participants' failure to infer a generative model for VSTM contents
recency bias reveals the nature of internal representations used during the task
